text: The bucket's raw_html_path and track_b_row for 'Nymeria' point to the historical Princess Nymeria of the Rhoynar (sources/wiki/_raw/Nymeria.json), not Arya's direwolf. The correct page is sources/wiki/_raw/Nymeria_(direwolf).json. I built the node from the direwolf page and ignored the track_b_row data (which contained titles like Princess of Dorne, allegiances to Rhoynar/House Martell, etc.). The triage script should map this bucket entry to 'Nymeria_(direwolf)' instead of 'Nymeria'. Node emitted as nymeria-direwolf.node.md using wiki infobox data from the correct page.
resolution: Confirmed. Triage script fixed via DIREWOLF_PAGE_OVERRIDES = {'Nymeria': 'Nymeria (direwolf)'} in scripts/wiki-pass2-triage.py — bucket now resolves to the correct page on regeneration. Existing nymeria-direwolf.node.md was already built from the correct cache file via agent self-correction; accepted as-is, no re-emit performed.

text: The Dragonkeepers are classified as organization.house in the bucket input, but they are an order of guards (dragon protectors), not a noble house. Reclassified to organization.faction in the emitted node. Confirm this is correct or if a different type is preferred.
resolution: Confirmed: organization.faction. Order of guards (dragon protectors), not a noble house. Agent reclassification accepted (Matt authorized in Session 23).

text: The track_b_row.first_available field for Sansa Stark reports ASOS chapter 1 (Prologue), but her cite_refs include AGOT chapters 4, 6, 7, 14, 15, 16, etc. Sansa clearly first appears in AGOT, not ASOS. This appears to be a parser bug in the first_available calculation — the parser may be picking up the wrong minimum cite_ref. The node was written with the parser's value verbatim per the agent rules, but the value is almost certainly wrong.
resolution: Set first_available to "always available" on Sansa node. New rule: positive-suspicion (agent has clear evidence parser value is wrong) → "always available"; otherwise default null. Update node frontmatter manually.

text: Parser first_available says AFFC ch.2 but cite_refs show AGOT ch.3, 57, 71 and pass1_mentions confirm AGOT appearances. Set to 'always available'. text: Track B parser extracted SPOUSE_OF target as "Weeping Water" — the Weeping Water is a river, not a person. The actual spouse was an unnamed miller who lived by the Weeping Water. The SPOUSE_OF edge was omitted from the node. This is a systematic parser bug: the infobox says "Miller of Weeping Water" and the parser took the linked entity (the river) rather than the described person.

The track_b parser encoded the infobox Mother field as PARENT_OF (reverse) with target "Godric Borrell", but the wiki HTML actually reads "Mother: Daughter of Godric Borrell". Gella's mother is an unnamed daughter of Godric, making Godric her maternal grandfather, not her parent. The node uses ANCESTOR_OF (Godric -> Gella) instead of PARENT_OF. This is a known parser limitation: the parser extracts the linked entity but loses the "Daughter of" wrapper text.

This page is a TWOW chapter summary (entity_type_guess: 'skip', skip_reason: 'category-skip'), not a battle. It was incorrectly included in the battles-a-b bucket. No node emitted. The triage script should exclude pages with entity_type_guess='skip' from battle buckets.

This page is a TWOW chapter summary (entity_type_guess: 'skip', skip_reason: 'category-skip'), not a battle. It was incorrectly included in the battles-a-b bucket. No node emitted. The triage script should exclude pages with entity_type_guess='skip' from battle buckets.

page: A Storm of Swords-Chapter 71 (and 16 other chapter-summary pages)
text: The battles-a bucket contains 17 chapter-summary pages (ASOS chapters 71-80, 8-9, Prologue, Epilogue; TWOW Alayne I, Arianne I, Arianne II) that were included with entity_type_guess=skip and skip_reason=category-skip. These are wiki chapter-summary articles, not battle/war/event entities. There is no valid entity type in the taxonomy for chapter summaries. The hard constraint says one node per page, but producing nodes for these would create graph entries with no meaningful type. Nodes were NOT emitted for these 17 pages. If chapter-summary pages should get nodes (e.g., as a future chapter/narrative type), the taxonomy needs an addition. Otherwise, the triage script should exclude these pages from event/battle buckets.

The track_b_row SPOUSE_OF edge for Tommard Heddle targets "Ambrose Butterwell" but the wiki infobox actually says "Eldest daughter of Ambrose Butterwell" (an unnamed character). The parser extracted the linked page name (Ambrose) rather than the relationship description. The SPOUSE_OF edge in the emitted node targets the unnamed daughter, not Ambrose. This is a systematic parser issue: when the spouse field links to a different entity than the actual spouse, the parser captures the link target.

The track_b_row types Caltrops as event.war, but the wiki page describes a conspiracy of 13 named nobles during the Dance of the Dragons who plotted to kill the Two Betrayers (Hugh Hammer and Ulf White). The wiki categories include "Organizations". Reclassified to organization.faction in the emitted node. This is a group of conspirators, not a war or battle.
